channel.title: Comments for page "Primero De Enero, 1856"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Primero De Enero, 1856"

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article comes from Wikimedia Commons, and is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wouldn't the guy eventually age?

Or get better at being a supervillain?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally I don't think he's aware that he's in a time loop, that's why it works at all

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh wow, I didn't realize it was a full time loop. :D This is even better!

Am I wrong for getting the idea that Santa Anna was slowly getting tired of constantly coming back to life?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I wanted to do was have people click back and forth between the two documents to get across the idea that it's a self-sustaining time loop. I like it this way - it does no harm and it's not like the upvotes for the second page count for the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is amazing. It probably should be merged with your 4000 proposal, but its still fucking amazing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel you could merge this and the 4k entry saying that this documents were recieved when the Society joined the Foundation. Having to pages feels unnecessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A note on the religious bend of the first few lines of this document: Benito Juárez was steadfast in his belief that the Catholic church should be less powerful than any branch of the government, but this specific document was ultimately not written by him. It's hard to get this idea across in the text, though maybe ultimately it doesn't matter and I'm worrying about nothing. Enjoy anyway.
